@@ -193,7 +193,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hvg","title":"Add Tailscale to doctor checks","description":"## Task Add Tailscale status to `acfs doctor` output. ## Doctor Check Implementation In `scripts/lib/doctor.sh`, add Tailscale check: ```bash check_tailscale() { echo "Tailscale:" # Binary check if ! command -v tailscale &>/dev/null; then echo " ✗ Not installed" return 1 fi local version version=$(tailscale version | head -1) echo " ✓ Installed: $version" # Daemon check if systemctl is-active --quiet tailscaled 2>/dev/null; then echo " ✓ Daemon: running" else echo " ✗ Daemon: not running" echo " Fix: sudo systemctl start tailscaled" return 1 fi # Connection check local status status=$(tailscale status --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.BackendState // "unknown"') case "$status" in "Running") echo " ✓ Status: connected" local ip hostname ip=$(tailscale ip -4 2>/dev/null) hostname=$(tailscale status --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.Self.HostName // "unknown"') echo " ✓ Tailscale IP: $ip" echo " ✓ Hostname: $hostname" ;; "NeedsLogin") echo " ⚠ Status: needs authentication" echo " Fix: sudo tailscale up" ;; "Stopped") echo " ⚠ Status: stopped" echo " Fix: sudo tailscale up" ;; *) echo " ⚠ Status: $status" ;; esac return 0 } ``` ## Output Example ``` $ acfs doctor ... Tailscale: ✓ Installed: 1.56.1 ✓ Daemon: running ✓ Status: connected ✓ Tailscale IP: 100.64.x.y ✓ Hostname: my-vps ... ``` ## Considerations - Don't fail doctor if Tailscale not authenticated (it's optional) - But DO show clear instructions on how to authenticate - Consider adding network connectivity test (ping another tailscale node?)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:58:17.753893-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:37:07.719226-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:37:07.719226-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented as part of bt5 - Tailscale check exists in doctor.sh (network.tailscale checks for installed/running/connected status)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hvg","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bt5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:15.295036-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hy7z","title":"TASK: Integrate SSH key prompt into installer main flow","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp (Installer SSH Key Integration) ## Summary Call the \`prompt_ssh_key()\` function at the appropriate point in install.sh, BEFORE the user normalization phase so the key can be migrated to ubuntu user. ## Implementation Details ### Integration Point In \`install.sh\`, add the SSH key prompt after initial setup but before normalize_user: \`\`\`bash # Near the start of main installation flow, after sourcing libs # Step 0: SSH Key Setup (if running as root with no key) if [[ \$EUID -eq 0 ]]; then source_lib "user.sh" prompt_ssh_key || true # Continue even if user skips/fails fi # Step 1: User normalization (existing) # This includes migrate_ssh_keys() which copies root's key to ubuntu normalize_user \`\`\` ### Why This Location 1. **Before normalize_user**: The migrate_ssh_keys() function inside normalize_user copies keys from /root/.ssh to /home/ubuntu/.ssh. We need the key installed FIRST. 2. **After sourcing libs**: We need the logging functions and prompt_ssh_key available. 3. **Root check**: Only prompt if running as root (the password-first flow). ### Error Handling The \`|| true\` ensures the installer continues even if: - User skips the key setup - Key validation fails - Any other issue with the prompt Users can always add their key later manually. ### Testing 1. Run full installer as root without existing key → should see prompt 2. Run full installer with existing key → should skip 3. After installer, verify ubuntu user has the key: \`cat /home/ubuntu/.ssh/authorized_keys\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] SSH key prompt appears early in installation - [ ] Prompt happens BEFORE normalize_user - [ ] Key is migrated to ubuntu user after prompt - [ ] Installer continues if user skips - [ ] Installer continues if key validation fails - [ ] No changes to existing flow for users with keys ## Dependencies - TASK: Implement prompt_ssh_key() function (must exist first) ## Files Modified - install.sh (add call to prompt_ssh_key)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:19:27.679097-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:33:33.337938-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:33:33.337938-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: prompt_ssh_key call present in install.sh normalize_user flow","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hy7z","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6y4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:19:53.184827-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hznw","title":"Task: Add cross-links between wizard and lessons","description":"# Add Cross-Links Between Wizard and Lessons ## Parent Feature Wizard Integration (mna) ## Goal Create bidirectional links between wizard steps and relevant lessons to help users navigate between the two learning paths. ## Wizard → Lessons Links Add "Learn more" sections to wizard steps that connect to deeper lesson content: | Wizard Step | Related Lesson | Link Text | |-------------|----------------|-----------| | Step 6: SSH Into Your VPS | Lesson 2: SSH & Persistence | "Struggling with SSH? [Learn SSH fundamentals →](/learn/ssh-basics)" | | Step 7: Set Up Accounts | Lesson 4: Agent Commands | "Need help with agent auth? [See Agent Commands →](/learn/agent-commands)" | | Step 10: Reconnect as Ubuntu | Lesson 1: Linux Navigation | "New to Linux? [Learn Linux basics →](/learn/linux-basics)" | | Step 11: Status Check | Lesson 7: Flywheel Loop | "Want to understand the tools? [See the complete workflow →](/learn/flywheel-loop)" | ### Implementation Use the existing `SimplerGuide` / `GuideTip` patterns: ```tsx // In wizard step component Want to learn more?{" "} After setup, check out our{" "} SSH fundamentals lesson {" "} for a deeper understanding. ``` ## Lessons → Wizard Links Add contextual back-links in lessons for users who haven't completed setup: ```tsx // In lesson component (conditional based on wizard completion) {!isWizardComplete && (

Not set up yet?{" "} Complete the setup wizard first →

)} ``` ### Relevant Back-Links | Lesson | Relevant Wizard Step | |--------|---------------------| | Lesson 2: SSH Basics | Step 6: SSH Into Your VPS | | Lesson 4: Agent Commands | Step 7: Set Up Accounts | | Lesson 0: Welcome | Step 12: Launch Onboarding | ## Shared Navigation Component Consider creating a shared component for contextual navigation: ```tsx // components/related-content.tsx interface RelatedContentProps { wizardStep?: { slug: string; title: string }; lesson?: { slug: string; title: string }; workflowSection?: string; } export function RelatedContent({ wizardStep, lesson, workflowSection }: RelatedContentProps) { return (

Related Content

    {wizardStep && (
  • 📋 Setup: {wizardStep.title}
    • )} {lesson && (
  • 📚 Lesson: {lesson.title}
    • )} {workflowSection && (
  • 🔄 Advanced: {workflowSection}
    • )}
); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] At least 4 wizard steps link to relevant lessons - [ ] Lessons show "Not set up?" banner for incomplete users - [ ] Links use consistent styling - [ ] Navigation is helpful, not overwhelming - [ ] Mobile-friendly ## File Changes - Update: Several `apps/web/app/wizard/*/page.tsx` files - Update: Lesson pages to include back-links - Create: `apps/web/components/related-content.tsx` {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-itb","title":"[ubu.12] Add snapshot recommendation and safety warnings","description":"# Add Snapshot Recommendation and Safety Warnings ## Purpose OS upgrades are one-way operations. Users should be clearly warned and encouraged to snapshot before proceeding. ## Pre-Upgrade Warning Message Display before starting any upgrade: ```bash ubuntu_show_upgrade_warning() { local current=$(ubuntu_get_version_string) local target="$TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION" local path=$(ubuntu_calculate_upgrade_path "$target") local hops=$(echo "$path" | wc -w) local estimated_time=$((hops * 45)) cat < ## DigitalOcean 1. Open Droplets dashboard 2. Click on your Droplet 3. Go to "Snapshots" tab 4. Click "Take Snapshot" ``` ## Confirmation for Interactive Mode For users not using --yes: ```bash ubuntu_confirm_upgrade() { if [[ "$YES_MODE" == "true" ]]; then log_info "Auto-confirmed (--yes mode)" return 0 fi ubuntu_show_upgrade_warning echo "" echo "Have you taken a snapshot? (Recommended but not required)" echo "" read -p "Proceed with Ubuntu upgrade? [y/N] " response if [[ ! "$response" =~ ^[Yy] ]]; then log_info "Upgrade cancelled by user" return 1 fi return 0 } ``` ## Non-Interactive Mode When running with --yes, still show warning but proceed: ```bash # In --yes mode, warning is informational only ubuntu_show_upgrade_warning log_warn "Proceeding automatically (--yes mode)" log_warn "If you need to abort, press Ctrl+C within 10 seconds" sleep 10 ``` ## Rollback Information Provide clear guidance that there is NO rollback: ```bash ubuntu_explain_no_rollback() { cat < {/* Optional: Top nav breadcrumb */}
← Back to Learning Hub
{/* Main content */}
{children}
); } ``` #### 2. Lesson Sidebar (`components/lesson-sidebar.tsx`) ```typescript interface LessonSidebarProps { currentSlug: string; } export function LessonSidebar({ currentSlug }: LessonSidebarProps) { const [completed] = useCompletedLessons(); return (
{LESSONS.map(lesson => { const isComplete = completed.includes(lesson.id); const isCurrent = lesson.slug === currentSlug; return ( {isComplete ? "✓" : isCurrent ? "●" : "○"} {lesson.title} ); })}
); } ``` #### 3. Lesson Navigation (`components/lesson-nav.tsx`) ```typescript interface LessonNavProps { currentId: number; onMarkComplete: () => void; isComplete: boolean; } export function LessonNav({ currentId, onMarkComplete, isComplete }: LessonNavProps) { const prev = getLessonById(currentId - 1); const next = getLessonById(currentId + 1); return (
{prev ? ( ) :
} {next ? ( ) : ( )}
); } ``` ### Responsive Behavior - **Desktop**: Two-column layout with sidebar - **Tablet**: Sidebar collapses to top nav or hamburger - **Mobile**: No sidebar, just content + bottom nav ### Reading Progress Indicator (Optional Enhancement) - Show scroll progress bar at top - Auto-mark complete when user scrolls to bottom? ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Layout renders correctly on `/learn/[slug]` pages - [ ] Sidebar shows all 9 lessons with status - [ ] Current lesson highlighted in sidebar - [ ] Previous/Next navigation works correctly - [ ] "Mark Complete" button toggles completion - [ ] Last lesson shows "Continue to Workflow" instead of Next - [ ] Responsive on all screen sizes ## File Changes - Create: `apps/web/app/learn/layout.tsx` - Create: `apps/web/components/lesson-sidebar.tsx` - Create: `apps/web/components/lesson-nav.tsx`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:20:31.097728-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:13.512264-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:13.512264-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in lesson-content.tsx as part of tor: desktop sidebar, lesson list with completion status, current highlight, prev/next nav, mark complete, responsive mobile nav","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-j9a","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tor","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:07.727673-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-j9a","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ori","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:08.278443-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} @@ -207,7 +207,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kwa","title":"Fail-fast remote curl|bash invocations in install.sh","description":"Several remote installer invocations in install.sh run via subshells without pipefail (e.g., oh-my-zsh uses sh -c with curl output, and multiple run_as_target bash -c "curl ... | sh"). If curl fails, the subshell can still return success and the installer continues. Fix by running these under bash with set -euo pipefail and passing args safely, so download/exec failures abort correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:17:16.089137-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:20:45.480511-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T15:20:45.480511-05:00","close_reason":"Wrap remote installer subshells with set -euo pipefail so curl failures abort; also make stack remote installer helper pass args safely and avoid masking download errors.","labels":["installer","reliability"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kwk","title":"Vendor NTM command_palette.md for onboarding","description":"# Task: Vendor NTM Command Palette ## Description Copy and maintain a local version of NTM's command_palette.md for use in onboarding. ## Background The onboard TUI needs to show NTM's prompt palette to users. Rather than fetch it live (requires internet), we vendor a copy. ## Implementation ### Vendor Location `acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md` ### Sync Script `scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh` ```bash #!/bin/bash # Sync NTM command palette from upstream SOURCE="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/command_palette.md" DEST="acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md" curl -fsSL "$SOURCE" > "$DEST" echo "Synced command_palette.md" ``` ### Install Location Deployed to: `~/.acfs/docs/ntm/command_palette.md` ### Onboard Integration In Mission 6, show the palette using: - bat (pretty) if available - less as fallback ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Sync script works - [ ] Palette included in installer - [ ] Displayed in onboard Mission 6 - [ ] Readable over SSH","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:28:15.931606-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:22:39.515282-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:22:39.515282-05:00","close_reason":"Vendored NTM command_palette.md to acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/. Added sync script scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh with --check mode. Commit 2a09af8.","labels":["onboard","task"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kyhv","title":"TASK: Add lesson completion celebration screen","description":"# TASK: Add lesson completion celebration screen ## Context When user completes a lesson, show a brief celebration before moving to next. ## Proposed Screen ``` ╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮ │ 🎉 Lesson Complete: tmux Basics │ │ │ │ You learned: │ │ • Creating sessions with ntm new │ │ • Attaching/detaching with Ctrl+B, D │ │ • Listing sessions with ntm list │ │ │ │ Progress: ████████████░░░░░░░░ 5/9 (56%) │ │ │ │ [Enter] Continue to next lesson │ │ [m] Return to menu │ ╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯ ``` ## Implementation ### Lesson Summary Data Add to each lesson markdown a summary section: ```markdown ``` Or define in onboard.sh as associative array. ### Celebration Function ```bash onboard_show_completion() { local lesson_num=$1 local lesson_title=$(get_lesson_title $lesson_num) local summary=$(get_lesson_summary $lesson_num) # Render celebration screen # Wait for keypress } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Celebration shown after lesson completion - [ ] Shows lesson title and summary points - [ ] Shows updated progress bar - [ ] Enter continues, m returns to menu - [ ] Works with gum and plain fallback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:59:49.050712-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:23:43.155042-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:23:43.155042-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented celebration screen with lesson summaries, progress bar, gum UI with fallback, and next/menu navigation","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kyhv","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-gjnn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T15:00:11.532879-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l1rl","title":"Task: Create Troubleshooting page for common issues","description":"","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:01.286219-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:01.286219-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l1rl","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vsg0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:53:57.855209-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l1rl","title":"Task: Create Troubleshooting page for common issues","description":"","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:01.286219-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:28:55.871206-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l1rl","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vsg0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:53:57.855209-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l2w","title":"Harden sync_ntm_palette.sh: HTTPS-only curl + single-fetch hashing","description":"scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh currently uses plain curl -fsSL and may fetch the remote file twice when computing the hash. Align with scripts/lib/security.sh (HTTPS-only curl args + enforce_https) and compute remote hash from a single fetch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDog","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:46:02.630169-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:51:03.294991-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T20:51:03.294991-05:00","close_reason":"Resolved on main: sync_ntm_palette.sh now uses scripts/lib/security.sh for HTTPS-only curl + single-fetch hashing (commit d683f1f). Shellcheck clean.","labels":["scripts","security"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l82e","title":"Task: Add installation output interpretation guide","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:47:20.770441-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:15:43.950908-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:15:43.950908-05:00","close_reason":"Addressed in r1z5 and existing run-installer page content: SSH disconnect warning (already present), installation output interpretation (added), curl command explanation (already present)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l82e","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r1z5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:54.359928-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lbo","title":"doctor: include user tool dirs in PATH","description":"scripts/lib/doctor.sh relies on PATH-only lookups, so running `acfs doctor` in a non-zshrc environment (fresh ssh, bash shell, etc.) can falsely report missing tools that are installed under ~/.local/bin, ~/.bun/bin, ~/.cargo/bin, etc. Make doctor self-contained by prepending common ACFS install dirs to PATH before checks (and optionally improve version detection for commands like go/tmux).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:46:21.82316-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T18:18:48.406739-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T18:18:48.406739-05:00","close_reason":"doctor self-contained PATH + version handling"} @@ -330,11 +330,11 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-va8","title":"[codex.6] Regenerate installer scripts after fixes","description":"## Goal After fixing the source files (doctor.sh, agents.sh), regenerate all derived scripts. ## Why Needed The manifest generator creates scripts from the source libraries. Any fixes to: - scripts/lib/doctor.sh - scripts/lib/agents.sh Must be followed by regenerating: - scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh - scripts/generated/install_agents.sh ## Commands cd packages/manifest && bun run generate ## Verification After regeneration: 1. Grep generated files for OPENAI_API_KEY - should find none related to codex auth 2. Review the generated scripts for correct codex auth guidance ## Depends On - codex.1 (doctor.sh fixes) - codex.2 (agents.sh fixes) ## Acceptance Criteria 1. All generated scripts reflect source file changes 2. No stale OPENAI_API_KEY references in generated codex checks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:02.623412-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:41:00.602163-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:41:00.602163-05:00","close_reason":"Regenerated all installer scripts. No OPENAI_API_KEY references for codex in generated files.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-va8","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ua5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:36.575242-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-va8","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-epc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:37.491646-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-va8","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yn6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:37.681275-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vmg","title":"Fix wizard step 5->6 navigation bug (page reload)","description":"User reported clicking 'SSH into your VPS' button on step 5 reloads the page instead of navigating. Root cause: ssh-connect page redirects back to create-vps if no IP stored. Fixed by: 1) Hiding Next button on step 5 (page has its own validated Continue button), 2) Scrolling to IP input if stepper is clicked, 3) Removed all window.alert() calls. Also fixed Contabo SSH key docs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T15:15:41.35079-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:15:48.703623-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:15:48.703623-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed in commits 246d272, d0e852e. Wizard step 5 no longer shows confusing Next button; navigation validates IP presence; window.alert replaced with scroll-to-element; Contabo docs updated."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vqr","title":"Create SSH troubleshooting guide","description":"# Task: Create SSH Troubleshooting Guide ## Description Comprehensive guide for common SSH connection failures. ## Location - Website: Embedded in wizard step 6 - Installer: ~/.acfs/docs/ssh_troubleshooting.md ## Common Issues Covered ### Permission denied (publickey) **Symptoms:** `Permission denied (publickey)` **Causes:** 1. Wrong key file specified 2. Key not added to VPS 3. Wrong username **Fixes:** ```bash # Verify key exists ls -la ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519* # Try with verbose output ssh -v -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@IP ``` ### Connection refused **Symptoms:** `Connection refused` **Causes:** 1. SSH service not running 2. Firewall blocking port 22 3. Wrong IP address **Fixes:** - Check IP is correct - VPS provider may need firewall rule ### Connection timeout **Symptoms:** `Connection timed out` **Causes:** 1. Wrong IP 2. VPS not fully booted 3. Network issue **Fixes:** - Wait 2-3 minutes for VPS to boot - Verify IP in provider dashboard ### Host key verification failed **Symptoms:** `Host key verification failed` **Causes:** - VPS was recreated with same IP - Man-in-the-middle warning **Fixes:** ```bash ssh-keygen -R IP_ADDRESS ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All common issues documented - [ ] Clear cause → fix structure - [ ] Commands are copy-paste ready - [ ] Integrated in website step 6","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:39:03.569312-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:30:24.161611-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:30:24.161611-05:00","close_reason":"Created Includes search, category filters, and expandable solutions.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vsg0","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:44:55.289914-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vsg0","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r1z5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:45:00.545891-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vsg0","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-sk9c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:45:05.794152-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vsg0","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9dl9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:45:11.03933-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vsg0","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r6xz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:45:16.290036-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vwv","title":"Build ERROR_PATTERNS database with suggested fixes","description":"# Task: Build ERROR_PATTERNS database with suggested fixes ## Context Part of EPIC: Per-Phase Error Reporting (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fkf) ## What to Do Create a database of known error patterns and their fixes: ### Error Patterns to Include ```bash declare -A ERROR_PATTERNS=( ["curl: (7) Failed to connect"]="Network issue. Check: curl -I https://google.com" ["curl: (6) Could not resolve"]="DNS failed. Check: cat /etc/resolv.conf" ["E: Unable to locate package"]="Package not found. Try: sudo apt-get update" ["Permission denied"]="Permission issue. Ensure running as root or with sudo." ["No space left on device"]="Disk full. Free up space: df -h" ["gpg: keyserver receive failed"]="GPG keyserver unreachable. Retry or check firewall." ["Connection timed out"]="Network timeout. Check firewall for outbound HTTPS." ["checksum mismatch"]="Upstream script changed. See GitHub issues." ["rate limit"]="API rate limited. Wait 60 seconds and retry." ) ``` ### get_suggested_fix() Function Match error text against patterns and return fix suggestion. ## Acceptance Criteria - At least 10 common error patterns covered - get_suggested_fix() returns actionable text - Fallback message for unknown errors - Easy to extend with new patterns ## Files to Create - scripts/lib/errors.sh","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:43:59.387392-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:39:10.792761-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:39:10.792761-05:00","close_reason":"Created scripts/lib/errors.sh with 33 error patterns and get_suggested_fix() function. All tests pass."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vydu","title":"[EPIC] Installation Resilience & Progress Feedback","description":"# Epic: Installation Resilience & Progress Feedback ## Problem Statement The 10-15 minute installation is anxiety-inducing for beginners: 1. **SSH disconnection panic**: If their WiFi hiccups, they lose connection and think they broke the VPS. They don't know the installer keeps running. 2. **Wall of text confusion**: Lots of output scrolling by. They don't know if it's working, failing, or what any of it means. 3. **curl | bash skepticism**: Security-aware users worry about piping curl to bash. The "View source" link exists but isn't prominent enough. 4. **Command gibberish**: `-fsSL` means nothing to them. ## Background & Reasoning Long-running installations are inherently risky for beginners. Any unexpected behavior triggers panic. The installer IS idempotent and resilient, but users don't know that. We need to: 1. Set expectations BEFORE they run the command 2. Explain what they'll see DURING installation 3. Provide clear recovery instructions IF something goes wrong 4. Address security concerns proactively ## User Story As a beginner running the installer, I want to understand what's happening and what to do if something goes wrong, So that I can wait patiently and recover from any issues. ## Success Criteria 1. User knows that disconnection is recoverable (just SSH back in) 2. User understands progress indicators (green checkmarks = good) 3. User knows to wait for "Installation complete" message 4. User can find and understand the install script source 5. User understands the curl command parts at a high level ## Scope - Add disconnection recovery instructions prominently - Add "What you'll see" section explaining output format - Make "View source" more prominent for security-conscious users - Add optional command breakdown for curious users - Consider adding a "If something fails" troubleshooting section ## Dependencies - Epic D: First SSH Connection (user must be connected) ## UI/UX Requirements - Maintain current polish for desktop and mobile - Disconnection warning should be AlertCard variant="warning" - Output explanation should use OutputPreview component - Source link should be visually distinct (security/trust badge) ## Technical Approach 1. Add AlertCard about disconnection recovery near top 2. Add OutputPreview showing example progress output 3. Add expandable "What is this command doing?" with breakdown 4. Enhance "View source" with trust badge styling 5. Add GuideCaution for "If installation seems stuck" ## Estimated Effort Small-Medium (1 wizard page enhancement) ## Priority Justification P1 (High) - Installation failure/abandonment means complete user loss.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:35:47.401107-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:57:18.94433-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:57:18.94433-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented command breakdown section explaining curl flags, | bash piping, and --yes/--mode options. Also addresses curl | bash security concerns. Page already had disconnection recovery, progress indicators, and view source links. All success criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vydu","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q7r3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:37:59.599308-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-w0q","title":"install.sh: dry-run prints misleading completion banner","description":"In --dry-run mode, install.sh skips all phases but still prints the full 'ACFS Installation Complete' summary. Adjust print_summary (or main) to show a dry-run summary instead, clearly stating no changes were made.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:56:39.819372-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:57:24.752126-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:57:24.752126-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed install.sh so --dry-run prints a dry-run completion banner (no changes made) instead of the full 'Installation Complete' summary."} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-w11f","title":"Task: Create comprehensive Glossary page","description":"","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:51:55.863317-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:51:55.863317-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-w11f","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vsg0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:53:52.570541-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-w11f","title":"Task: Create comprehensive Glossary page","description":"","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:51:55.863317-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:28:24.220413-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:28:24.220413-05:00","close_reason":"Glossary already comprehensive: 68 terms in lib/jargon.ts, two glossary pages (/glossary and /learn/glossary) with search, category filtering, hash navigation, related terms, and Learn More links. TypeScript verification passed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-w11f","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vsg0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:53:52.570541-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-w6gf","title":"Task: Add curl command explanation with security context","description":"","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:47:26.16767-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:15:43.951402-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:15:43.951402-05:00","close_reason":"Addressed in r1z5 and existing run-installer page content: SSH disconnect warning (already present), installation output interpretation (added), curl command explanation (already present)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-w6gf","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r1z5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:59.646036-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-wfi2","title":"Add installation progress feedback explanation","description":"# Task: Add Installation Progress Feedback Explanation ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Installation Resilience & Progress Feedback ## Problem During the 10-15 minute installation, users see a "wall of text" scrolling by. They don't know: - Is it working or failing? - What do the colors mean? - How far along are we? - When should I worry? ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/run-installer/page.tsx Add "What You'll See" section: \`\`\`tsx

The installer shows progress as it works. Here's how to read it:

✓

Green checkmarks = Success

That step completed successfully.

!

Yellow warnings = Minor issue

Something optional was skipped. Usually fine.

✗

Red X = Step failed

The installer will retry or work around it.

Lots of scrolling text is normal! Just watch for the final "Installation complete" message. If you see mostly green checkmarks, you're good. \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explains color coding (green/yellow/red) - [ ] Shows visual examples of each state - [ ] Reassures that scrolling text is normal - [ ] Tells user what to wait for (final message) ## Why This Matters Anxiety during installation leads to premature abandonment or unnecessary panic. Users who understand the progress indicators can wait patiently.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:54:50.363193-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:35.446502-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:35.446502-05:00","close_reason":"Already covered: run-installer/page.tsx explains progress messages and idempotent behavior"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-whw3","title":"Update README OS requirement to Ubuntu 25.10","description":"README still references Ubuntu 24.04+ in badges and diagrams. Update to reflect current requirement: target Ubuntu 25.10 with auto-upgrade from 22.04+ (per AGENTS.md).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:52:58.302284-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:54:59.003219-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:54:59.003219-05:00","close_reason":"Updated README Ubuntu requirement to 25.10"}
